_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 9-10R Acetaminophen (Revised 12/10/2025, MDHHS Reviewed 2026). Base Michigan template.
protocol_reference: Michigan Medication Protocol Section 9-10R: Acetaminophen — MDHHS Approved 1/5/2026, Reviewed 2026
contraindications: Severe acute liver disease or hepatic failure
precautions: Caution in patients with chronic liver disease or history of alcohol abuse
Ask about recent acetaminophen use — avoid exceeding daily maximum
Children <60 days: rectal temperature must be documented before administration
expected_effects: Reduction in fever (antipyretic effect within 30-60 minutes)
side_effects: Hepatotoxicity with overdose or in patients with liver impairment
notes: Children <60 days: rectal temperature must be documented
Adult dose is for >14 years old; pediatric for ≤14 years old per Michigan definition
Liquid pediatric formulation: 160 mg/5 mL standard concentration — use dosing table by weight and age

dosing.dose: Per MI-MEDIC or weight/age-based dosing table
dosing.notes: Standard liquid formulation: 160 mg/5 mL; dose by weight and age; children <60 days: document rectal temperature before administration
